document.title: lionscliapp Reference Guide
document.purpose: Complete reference for the lionscliapp framework, intended for LLM consumption.

what_it_provides: Persistence: creates a hidden project folder (e.g. .mytool/) with config.json; values set with 'mytool set key value' survive across invocations.
Automatic CLI parsing: declare keys, framework handles parsing/persisting/merging; values appear in app.ctx (a plain global dict) ready to use.
Multiple commands: bind sub-commands with declare_cmd; declare a default (no-command) handler for bare invocation.
Paths are native: any key prefixed with 'path.' is automatically converted to a pathlib.Path (expanded, resolved). read_json/write_json take path names directly.
JSON is native: read_json('input') and write_json('output', data) require no import json, no open/close, no path construction.
Built-in commands: set, get, keys, help, and help-basics are always available without any work. If uses_locking is enabled, unlock is also available.
what_it_does_not_do: No automatic type coercion for general keys (except path.* and json.indent.*). All other values are strings.
No built-in integer flags, boolean flags, enums, or validated choices. Coerce from app.ctx yourself.
No concurrency abstractions. Single-process, single-lifecycle by design.

design_principles.globals: app.ctx is global and honest about it. CLI tools run once, single process, single context.
design_principles.json_substrate: All declared data must be JSON-serializable. Configuration is JSON.
design_principles.no_classes: Core architecture uses plain dicts and named functions. Data and behavior kept separate.
design_principles.flags_as_splitters: Functions that do one thing in multiple ways take a short flag character. mode='c' means configured; mode='p' means project-relative.

keys_and_namespaces.overview: Keys are dot-namespaced strings declared with app.declare_key(key, default). All values land in app.ctx after merging.
keys_and_namespaces.values_are_strings: Values from CLI or 'set' command arrive as plain strings. Coerce yourself if needed: int(app.ctx['retry.count']).

execpath.*.meaning: Value is converted to pathlib.Path, resolved relative to the execution root (where the CLI is invoked).
coercion: Relative paths resolved against execution root

projpath.*.meaning: Value is converted to pathlib.Path, resolved relative to the project directory (e.g. .mytool/).
coercion: Relative paths resolved against project directory
projpath.*.example_usage: p = app.ctx['projpath.cache']  # pathlib.Path inside the project dir

path.*.meaning: Deprecated alias for execpath.*. Behaves identically. Use execpath.* or projpath.* in new code.
path.*.deprecation_note: declare_key() emits DeprecationWarning when a path.* key is declared. The key continues to work and resolves relative paths against execroot, same as execpath.*.

json.indent.*.meaning: Indentation level (coerced to int) for write_json output. 0 means compact (no whitespace).
json.indent.*.wired_into: write_json() in configured mode checks ctx['json.indent.<id>'] where <id> matches the write_json id argument.
json.indent.*.user_control: mytool set json.indent.results 0  (compact) | mytool set json.indent.results 4  (4-space indent)

namespaces.other: All other namespace prefixes: no coercion, identity passthrough. Values remain as-is.

order.source: CLI overrides (--key value on command line)

builtin_commands.name: set
builtin_commands.description: Persist a configuration value to config.json.

builtin_commands.name: get
builtin_commands.description: Show current state of a key (default, stored value, resolved value).

builtin_commands.name: keys
builtin_commands.description: List all declared configuration keys with short descriptions.

builtin_commands.name: help
builtin_commands.description: Show application help. Without argument: lists commands and options. With command name: detailed help.

builtin_commands.name: help-basics
builtin_commands.description: Show framework-oriented lionscliapp help, including built-in commands, configuration layering, and special options such as --execroot and --project-dir.

builtin_commands.name: unlock
builtin_commands.description: Remove a stale lock.json file from the project directory. Available only when uses_locking is true.

cli_global_options.description: Override a configuration value for this invocation only (not persisted).
Load batch configuration overrides from a JSON file (must contain 'options' object).
Override the execution root directory. Only accepted if allow_execroot_override is true.
Override the project directory name for this invocation. A directory name only (no slashes), not a full path. Only accepted if allow_projectdir_override is True.

declaration_functions.name: declare_app
declaration_functions.description: Set the application name and version string.

declaration_functions.name: describe_app
declaration_functions.description: Set a description for the application.

flags.description: Pass "" or "s" for short description (default); pass "l" for long description.

declaration_functions.example: app.describe_app('A tool that does useful things')  |  app.describe_app('Extended details...', 'l')

declaration_functions.name: declare_projectdir
declaration_functions.description: Set the name of the project directory where config.json is stored. Created under execution root on first run.

declaration_functions.name: declare_cmd
declaration_functions.description: Bind a command name to a Python callable (no arguments). Use empty string or None for no-command dispatch.
declaration_functions.note: Built-in names (set, get, help, help-basics, keys, and unlock when uses_locking is enabled) cannot be used.
declaration_functions.example:
app.declare_cmd("work", do_work)  # named command
app.declare_cmd("", do_default)   # no-command dispatch
declaration_functions.no_command_dispatch: To handle bare invocation (no command given), register a handler under the empty string: app.declare_cmd("", my_default_fn). If no handler is registered, bare invocation prints help automatically.

declaration_functions.name: describe_cmd
declaration_functions.description: Set a description for a command.

flags.description: Pass "" or "s" for short description (default); pass "l" for long description.

declaration_functions.name: set_cmd_flag
declaration_functions.description: Set a boolean flag on a specific command. This is used for command-level framework behavior such as locking.
declaration_functions.raises: ValueError if value is not a bool

known_flags.locking: bool, default False. If True, the command acquires lock.json before execution and releases it afterward.

declaration_functions.name: declare_key
declaration_functions.description: Declare a configuration key with its default value. Default must be JSON-serializable.
declaration_functions.note: For keys users will set from CLI or 'set', use string defaults since those values arrive as strings.

declaration_functions.name: describe_key
declaration_functions.description: Set a description for a configuration key.

flags.description: Pass "" or "s" for short description (default); pass "l" for long description.

declaration_functions.name: set_flag
declaration_functions.description: Set an application flag. Flags control framework behaviour such as whether certain CLI overrides are accepted.
declaration_functions.raises: ValueError if value is not a bool

known_flags.search_upwards_for_project_dir: bool, default False. Walk up directory tree to find project dir.
known_flags.allow_execroot_override: bool, default True. Accept --execroot CLI option.
known_flags.allow_projectdir_override: bool, default True. Accept --project-dir CLI option.
known_flags.uses_locking: bool, default False. Enable the locking subsystem and the built-in unlock command.

declaration_functions.name: declare
declaration_functions.description: Mass-declare by deep-merging a specification dictionary into the application model.
declaration_functions.merge_semantics: Dicts merged recursively; scalars last-value-wins. Does not bind command functions (use declare_cmd for that).
declaration_functions.note: callable fn values cannot appear in declare() spec; use declare_cmd() to bind functions.

entrypoint.name: main
entrypoint.description: Start the application. Always the last call in your script.
steps: Build app.ctx by merging all layers and coercing path.* keys
Acquire lock.json if the selected command has locking=True

exit_codes.1: Validation, configuration, or CLI parsing error

runtime_context.name: ctx
runtime_context.description: Plain dictionary containing merged, coerced configuration for the current invocation. Available during command execution only.
runtime_context.warning: Empty before app.main() runs. Do not read at module level or during declarations.

get_path.description: Resolve a path. Returns an absolute pathlib.Path.

modes.p: Project: resolves <id> relative to project directory
modes.e: Execroot: resolves <id> relative to execution root
modes.f: Filesystem: treats <id> as a literal path (absolute, or relative to cwd)

get_path.example:
app.get_path('output')             # ctx['path.output'] as Path
app.get_path('cache.json', 'p')   # <project_dir>/cache.json
app.get_path('data/in.json', 'e') # <execroot>/data/in.json

read_json.description: Read and parse a JSON file. Mode is a single path flag character (c/p/e/f). Format flags invalid.
read_json.example:
data = app.read_json('input')           # reads ctx['path.input'] file
data = app.read_json('state.json', 'p') # reads <project_dir>/state.json

write_json.description: Write Python data as JSON to a file. Creates parent directories. Adds trailing newline.
write_json.mode_string: One path flag (c/p/e/f) plus optional format flag (0 or 2). Order independent.
formatting_resolution: 1. Explicit format flag (0 or 2) always takes precedence.
2. For 'c' mode without format flag: check ctx['json.indent.<id>'] (0=compact, positive=indent level).
write_json.example:
app.write_json('output', results)        # configured path, ctx formatting
app.write_json('output', results, 'c2') # force pretty
app.write_json('output', results, 'c0') # force compact
app.write_json('cache.json', d, 'p2')  # project-relative, pretty
app.write_json('/tmp/dbg.json', d, 'f') # literal path

reset.description: Reset all global framework state to declaring-ready. Clears all declarations; phase returns to 'declaring'. For tests and interactive use.

StartupError.description: Raised by app.main() for pre-dispatch errors: validation failures, missing declarations, bad CLI syntax, config file problems.

DispatchError.description: Raised by app.main() when command name is not recognized or function is not bound.

search_upwards_for_project_dir.meaning: Walk up directory tree looking for project directory. Makes tool usable from subdirectories (like git finding .git).

allow_execroot_override.meaning: When true, --execroot CLI option is accepted.

allow_projectdir_override.meaning: When True, the --project-dir CLI option is accepted, allowing callers to override the project directory name for a single invocation. Value must be a plain directory name with no path separators.

minimal_complete_example.description: A small but complete tool demonstrating the framework.
code: app.describe_key('execpath.inbox',       'Where incoming files are read from')
